effortModel.citation: r/learnmath: routine 2-5min; trig substitution/improper 30-60min; r/calculus DP-33 Calc2 hard 30-48min

expectedStrategy: time-based approach: set 60min timer and solve as many as possible
easy problems first (u-sub, basic parts) — fast pass
30-minute cap per problem — flag and move on if stuck
office hours for problems stuck 30+ minutes
warnings: trig substitution and improper integrals can take 20-60min each — this is NORMAL
uniform '10 problems/day' plan ignores difficulty variance — do NOT use
alternatives: time-based: maximize problems solved in 60min sessions
flag genuinely stuck problems (30+ min) for office hours
hardFailChecks: must NOT create '10 problems/day' uniform plan — ignores difficulty
must NOT use fixed min/problem (e.g., 13min flat)
must validate 'really hard' — Calc 2 hard problems are objectively 30-60min each
must mention office hours for stuck problems
toneExpectation: frustration-validating; normalize hard problems with real data; practical time-based triage
